key_quote: No pointer/memory handling... addresses 'the easy half of C-to-Rust verification while leaving the hard half untouched'
rationale: All reviewers praise the σ-bridge abstraction and QF_BV encoding as clean, extensible, and novel. The critiques focus on execution depth: missing pointer/memory handling, 0% CEGAR success on UB, synthetic-only benchmarks, and uncharacterized scalability — all depth/completeness issues.
